page: Microsoft Azure EC32eds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECEDV6 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture, powered by an Intel processor, and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration includes 1890 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk attachments. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and there are no integrated graphics processing units on this instance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC32eds_v6 provides a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads requiring significant RAM without the expense of higher core counts. The combination of 256.0 GB of memory and 1890 GB of local storage makes this server suitable for memory-intensive applications, large databases, enterprise applications, and data caching layers.
description: Microsoft Azure EC32eds_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEDV6 family, powered by an Intel processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. For storage, the server is equipped with 1890 GB of local disk space and supports premium storage configurations. It does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the server offers high resource density for memory-heavy workloads, balancing substantial RAM with moderate compute resources. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including database hosting, enterprise applications, and caching systems.
